2008-02-01.title: Seeking Independence
2008-02-01.abstract: Nicaragua and Colombia both claim San Andrés in a bitter territorial dispute. Now, a nonviolent separatist movement is growing increasingly vocal.

2008-02-01.title: China Accuses Online Activist of Subversion
2008-02-01.abstract: International human rights groups say the arrest is part of a broader effort by authorities to crack down on dissidents before China hosts the Olympics in August.

2008-02-01.title: Indonesian Chickens, and People, Hard Hit by Bird Flu
2008-02-01.abstract: Three people died of bird flu last week, pushing the total number of deaths in Indonesia to 101, nearly half of all the bird flu deaths in the world.

2008-02-01.title: Kurds’ Power Wanes as Arab Anger Rises
2008-02-01.abstract: Once in a position of strength, Iraqi Kurds are pursuing policies that are uniting most Sunnis and many Shiites.

2008-02-01.title: Second Lawmaker Is Killed as Kenya’s Riots Intensify
2008-02-01.abstract: Kenyan government officials were quick to say the killing was a “crime of passion” connected to a love triangle. Opposition leaders called it an assassination.

2008-02-02.title: Worst Attack on Baghdad in Months
2008-02-02.abstract: Twin bombings struck two markets in central Baghdad on Friday, killing 65 people and wounding 149.

2008-02-02.title: Bombings in Baghdad Markets
2008-02-02.abstract: Attacks that killed dozens of people at two popular Iraqi pet markets were the bloodiest in six months.

2008-02-02.title: Bombings in Baghdad Markets
2008-02-02.abstract: Attacks that killed dozens of people at two popular Iraqi pet markets were the bloodiest in six months.

2008-02-02.title: Rebels Are Said to Enter Chad’s Capital
2008-02-02.abstract: There were reports of 1,000 to 1,500 rebels in Ndjamena and gun battles around the presidential palace.

2008-02-02.title: Two Bombings Wreak Carnage in Iraqi Capital
2008-02-02.abstract: Two women strapped with explosives killed dozens of people in the deadliest day in Baghdad in months.

2008-02-03.title: Training for Carnival
2008-02-03.abstract: More than ever, it is survival of the fittest at Rio’s renowned Carnival celebration — a full-tilt sport in high heels.

2008-02-03.title: No Body Left Untoned Preparing for Carnival
2008-02-03.abstract: More than ever, it is survival of the fittest at Rio’s renowned Carnival celebration — a full-tilt sport in high heels.

2008-02-03.title: Gun Battles in Chad’s Capital as Rebel Forces Storm In
2008-02-03.abstract: A rebel army swarmed Ndjamena in an attack that raised the specter of deeper chaos in the fragile region.

2008-02-03.title: Sunnis Say Law Opening Jobs to Ex-Baathists Would Do More Harm Than Good
2008-02-03.abstract: Top Sunni politicians, including Iraq’s vice president, say they are trying to stop final approval for legislation that they believe would actually hurt Sunnis’ chances of retaining government jobs.

2008-02-03.title: A Frail Economy Raises Pressure on Iran’s Rulers
2008-02-03.abstract: From fundamentalists to reformists, Iranians are talking more loudly about the need for a pragmatic approach.

2008-02-04.title: Cold Chinese Grow Angry Over Lack of Preparation
2008-02-04.abstract: The Chinese government in the last week has worked as hard at public relations and crisis management, faced with the crippling effects of some of the most important snowfall in 50 years.

2008-02-04.title: U.S. Says It Accidentally Killed 9 Iraqi Civilians
2008-02-04.abstract: American forces acknowledged what appeared to be one of the deadliest cases of mistaken identity in recent weeks.

2008-02-04.title: A Lebanese Diva, Performing in Syria, Creates Drama in More Ways Than One
2008-02-04.abstract: Fayrouz, the great Lebanese diva, returned to Damascus for the first time in three decades, a performance some considered a betrayal.

2008-02-04.title: Leak on Cross-Border Chases From Iraq
2008-02-04.abstract: American forces in Iraq were in some cases authorized to pursue foes into Iran and Syria, according to a classified 2005 document that was leaked.

2008-02-04.title: Israeli Defense Minister to Stay in Olmert Coalition
2008-02-04.abstract: The move stabilizes Prime Minister Ehud Olmert’s coalition despite last week’s harsh report on the handling of the Lebanon war in 2006.

2008-02-05.title: As a Japanese Island Grows Less Remote, a Wildcat Grows More Endangered
2008-02-05.abstract: Researchers worry that tourists may be endangering one the world’s rarest wildcats, which is found only on the Japanese island of Iriomote.

2008-02-05.title: Sexually Transmitted Diseases Common in Australian Aborigines, Report Shows
2008-02-05.abstract: A report says that sexually transmitted diseases have reached hyperendemic proportions in some Aboriginal communities and suggested mass treatment.

2008-02-05.title: Time Runs Out for an Afghan Held by the U.S.
2008-02-05.abstract: The fate of the first detainee to die of natural causes at Guantánamo Bay reveals the problems of military tribunals, Afghans say.

2008-02-05.title: U.N. Urges Outside Help for Chad
2008-02-05.abstract: The Security Council adopted a statement condemning attacks by rebels seeking to overthrow the government of Chad and urging other countries to help stop the violence.

2008-02-05.title: Processing of Iraqi Refugees Remains Slow, U.S. Says
2008-02-05.abstract: The process by which Iraqis who worked for the American Embassy in Baghdad can immigrate to the United States remains slow moving, the State Department said Monday.

2008-02-06.title: Challenger to Mugabe in Zimbabwe
2008-02-06.abstract: A senior member of Zimbabwe’s ruling party announced that he would run for president against Robert Mugabe.

2008-02-06.title: Sudan: Progress for Darfur Peace Force
2008-02-06.abstract: The United Nations secretary general, Ban Ki-moon, said that Bangladesh and Ethiopia had offered to lend helicopters to the new African Union-United Nations force for Darfur and that President Omar Hassan al-Bashir of Sudan had dropped objections to Thai and Nepalese troops. Just 9,000 of the projected 26,000 troops are in Darfur because of the Sudanese government’s reluctance to approve non-African soldiers and the unwillingness of military powers to meet repeated requests from Mr. Ban for the 18 attack and 6 transport helicopters the force will need. John Sawers, Britain’s ambassador to the United Nations, said the developments were welcome but added, “Anyone who dealt with Sudan over the last several years will be wary of announcements of progress.”.

2008-02-06.title: India’s School Shortage Means Glut of Parental Stress
2008-02-06.abstract: Admissions for prekindergarten seats in Delhi begin for children as young as 3, and what school they get into is widely felt to make or break their fate.

2008-02-06.title: Death Toll in Kenya Exceeds 1,000, but Talks Reach Crucial Phase
2008-02-06.abstract: According to the Red Cross, most of the killings have been in the turbulent Rift Valley, where gangs from opposing ethnic groups have fought fiercely.

2008-02-06.title: American Soldiers Kill 3 Iraqis in Raid
2008-02-06.abstract: According to the Iraqi police, the casualties were family members living in a tiny one-room house in Tikrit.

2008-02-07.title: Hundreds of Expatriates Flee Violence in Chad
2008-02-07.abstract: Evacuees recounted tales of shooting and mayhem after days of violence in Ndjamena.

2008-02-07.title: Europe Spars With Serbia Over Union and Kosovo
2008-02-07.abstract: Serbia’s prime minister criticized a political and economic agreement with the European Union, over what he called a “deception” aimed at tricking his country into conceding the independence of Kosovo.

2008-02-07.title: Iran: Russia Says New Rocket Raises Nuclear ‘Suspicions’
2008-02-07.abstract: Iran fired the rocket Monday from its new space center in the deserts of Semnan Province and said it wanted to launch domestically built satellites for research and telecommunications.

2008-02-07.title: With Flawed System Unchanged, Italy Sets Elections for April
2008-02-07.abstract: President Giorgio Napolitano took the action after political factions failed to agree on a plan to revise the country’s flawed electoral law.

2008-02-07.title: U.S.-Backed Russian Institutes Help Iran Build Reactor
2008-02-07.abstract: The two institutes received U.S. financing through a program intended to prevent impoverished scientists and their institutions from selling expertise to states or terrorist groups seeking nuclear weapons.

2008-02-08.title: C.I.A. Chief Doubts Tactic to Interrogate Is Still Legal
2008-02-08.abstract: The flurry of public statements about the use of waterboarding against terrorist suspects showed how the harsh tactics secretly adopted after Sept. 11 have become a stubborn political burden.

2008-02-08.title: Australia Shows Graphic Images of Japanese Whaling
2008-02-08.abstract: Australia’s government stepped up its campaign against Japanese whaling by releasing grisly surveillance pictures of the carcasses of a minke whale and a calf being hauled aboard a harpoon ship.

2008-02-08.title: Two Arrested in Bhutto Assassination
2008-02-08.abstract: Pakistani officials said that two suspects were arrested Thursday morning in Rawalpindi.

2008-02-08.title: No Quasimodo, He Brings Music to Notre-Dame Bells
2008-02-08.abstract: A 40-year-old chemist by training, Stéphane Urbain became the keeper of the bells of the Paris cathedral in 2005.

2008-02-08.title: New Leader Named for Greek Orthodox Church
2008-02-08.abstract: Metropolitan Ieronymos of Thebes, a seasoned cleric who has avoided conflict, has been elected the new leader of the Greek Orthodox Church.

2008-02-09.title: Conflict Hurts Kenya's Middle Class
2008-02-09.abstract: Torn between their work identity and their ethnic backgrounds, members of Kenya's vibrant middle class anxiously try to stay in business.

2008-02-09.title: Bhutto’s Widower Rallies Party for Election in Pakistan
2008-02-09.abstract: Asif Zardari opened his party’s election campaign with a huge rally in a bid to sweep to power on Benazir Bhutto’s legacy and martyrdom.

2008-02-09.title: Britain: Everyone’s Nightmare
2008-02-09.abstract: An inquest was opened into the death of an elderly man whose body lay on the sofa of his Bristol apartment for at least eight years while another elderly occupant continued to live there.

2008-02-09.title: Annan Sees Small Gains in Peace Talks in Kenya
2008-02-09.abstract: The former United Nations secretary general has spent the past week trying to nudge Kenya’s government and opposition leaders toward a compromise.

2008-02-09.title: In China, Scramble Continues in Coal Country
2008-02-09.abstract: The recent snowstorms that left millions without power in Southern China underscored the country’s heavy dependence on coal and laid bare the glaring inadequacies of its production and distribution cycle.

2008-02-10.title: Israelis Urge Tougher Action in Gaza
2008-02-10.abstract: Israeli cabinet ministers urged sharper military action in Gaza after a rocket attack by Palestinian militants seriously wounded two brothers.

2008-02-10.title: Conflicts Deepen Between Local Iraqi Governments and U.S.-Backed Sunni Groups
2008-02-10.abstract: The conflicts raise the prospect that the creation of the groups, formed to fight extremists and bring calm to the country, might instead add to the unrest in Diyala and Anbar provinces.

2008-02-10.title: Bhutto’s Widower Rallies Party
2008-02-10.abstract: The party of Benazir Bhutto hopes to sweep to power in Pakistan on her legacy and her martyrdom.

2008-02-10.title: Turkey’s Parliament Lifts Scarf Ban
2008-02-10.abstract: A vote in Parliament set the stage for a showdown with the secular elite over Islam’s place in Turkish society.

2008-02-10.title: Terror Threat From Pakistan Said to Expand
2008-02-10.abstract: A suspected bombing plot in Spain is indicative of an important threat coming from Pakistan, officials said.

2008-02-11.title: Conflict Hurts Kenya's Middle Class
2008-02-11.abstract: Torn between their work identity and their ethnic backgrounds, members of Kenya's vibrant middle class anxiously try to stay in business.

2008-02-11.title: In Mexico: A Bus of Women's Own
2008-02-11.abstract: Mexico City has created "Women Only" buses in response to complaints of sexual harassment on public transportation.

2008-02-11.title: Chávez Threatens to End Oil Exports to U.S. in Exxon Feud
2008-02-11.abstract: The warning ratchets up a fierce legal dispute between Venezuela and Exxon after President Hugo Chávez’s move to exert greater state control over the nation’s oil industry last year.

2008-02-11.title: Army Buried Study Faulting Iraq Planning
2008-02-11.abstract: The unclassified report identified problems with nearly every organization that had a role in planning the war, including the White House and the Defense Department.

2008-02-11.title: Kenya’s Middle Class Feeling Sting of Violence
2008-02-11.abstract: The well-established middle class is thought to be one of the factors that separates Kenya from other African countries that have been consumed by ethnic conflict.

2008-02-12.title: Twin Bombings in Baghdad
2008-02-12.abstract: Ian Fisher reports on two deadly blasts in the neighborhood of Karada.

2008-02-12.title: Taliban Commander Is Captured
2008-02-12.abstract: A senior Taliban commander was arrested by Pakistani forces as he tried to slip across the Afghan border into Pakistan.

2008-02-12.title: Israeli Casts Doubt on Pact This Year
2008-02-12.abstract: Israeli officials hope to reach agreement with the Palestinians on a “declaration of principles” for peace by the end of the year, but not on a detailed peace treaty, said a senior Israeli minister.

2008-02-12.title: Russia Forgives Iraq Billions in Debt
2008-02-12.abstract: Iraq’s foreign minister won a long-sought agreement to write off most of Iraq’s $12.9 billion in debt to Russia.

2008-02-12.title: $89 Million Needed in Southern Floods
2008-02-12.abstract: The United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs appealed for $89 million to aid 449,000 people in Mozambique, Malawi, Zambia and Zimbabwe hit by flooding. Halfway through the rainy season, thousands have seen their crops ruined and homes destroyed.

2008-02-13.title: Warding Off Evil Spirits, but Not Toll of Affluence
2008-02-13.abstract: Having survived typhoons, Japanese conquest and war, the religious traditions of Miyako Island in Japan are losing ground to an increasingly urban life.

2008-02-13.title: France: Sarkozy’s Son Not a Candidate
2008-02-13.abstract: President Nicolas Sarkozy’s 21-year-old son, Jean, will not be a candidate for local office next month in Neuilly, the wealthy Paris suburb that his father ran as mayor from 1983 to 2003, according to news reports. Speculation that he might run came after he turned against the president’s handpicked but unpopular candidate for mayor, David Martinon, after a poll suggested Mr. Martinon would lose the race, and he withdrew.

2008-02-13.title: Making a Case for a Pause in Troop Cutbacks in Iraq
2008-02-13.abstract: American commanders fear that security may rapidly deteriorate in Iraq if the U.S. overestimates the capability of the country’s forces to keep the peace.

2008-02-13.title: U.N. Weighs a Ban on Weapons in Space, but U.S. Still Objects
2008-02-13.abstract: At the United Nations Conference on Disarmament on Tuesday, the United States rejected a Russian-Chinese draft treaty banning weapons in space.

2008-02-13.title: New Pakistan Army Chief Orders Military Out of Civilian Government Agencies, Reversing Musharraf Policy
2008-02-13.abstract: The order by the army chief, Gen. Ashfaq Parvez Kayani, was the boldest step he has taken to disentangle the military from the civilian sphere of the government.

2008-02-14.title: In Tribal Pakistan, Religious Parties Are Foundering
2008-02-14.abstract: Since being swept to power in 2002 on a wave of anti-Americanism, religious parties in Pakistan have found the public mood has now shifted against them.

2008-02-14.title: Before bin Laden, One of World’s Most Feared Men
2008-02-14.abstract: Imad Mugniyah, who was killed on Wednesday, had been implicated in the hijacking of planes and in other devastating attacks against Americans, Israelis and others in the 1980s and ’90s.

2008-02-14.title: East Timor, Shaken by Attack on Leaders, Weighs Causes
2008-02-14.abstract: In the wake of Monday’s twinned attacks on the president and prime minister of East Timor, the United Nations and East Timorese police have begun a joint investigation.

2008-02-14.title: Strike Disrupts Greece as Workers Seek to Protect Their Pensions
2008-02-14.abstract: Greece’s pay-as-you-go pension system ranks among the most generous in the European Union, but barring changes it faces hundreds of billions of deficit within a decade.

2008-02-14.title: As Mores Evolve, India’s Divorced Seek Second Chance
2008-02-14.abstract: Marriage in India is still, by and large, socially compulsory. But in a measure of the slow churning of Indian social mores, divorce and remarriage are slowly gaining acceptability.

2008-02-15.title: The Segregation of Kenya
2008-02-15.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of Kenyans are being forced to resettle in ethnically homogeneous zones, redrawing the country's map.

2008-02-15.title: Two Gatherings in Beirut
2008-02-15.abstract: Robert F. Worth discusses the funeral of the Hezbollah leader Imad Mugniyah and the anniversary of the killing of  Rafik Hariri.

2008-02-15.title: As Kenyan Rivals Haggle, a Mother Mourns at 2 Graves
2008-02-15.abstract: Two children killed in one of the most disturbing episodes in the bloodletting in Kenya were buried on Thursday.

2008-02-15.title: China Denies Justice Department’s Accusations of Espionage
2008-02-15.abstract: The U.S. Justice Department had accused four people of passing military secrets to the Chinese government.

2008-02-15.title: China Rejects Darfur Criticism
2008-02-15.abstract: China said sports and politics should be kept separate, after filmmaker Steven Spielberg criticized Beijing for not using its influence to end the humanitarian crisis in Darfur.

2008-02-16.title: On the Stump, Pakistani Candidates Avoid Musharraf
2008-02-16.abstract: After 10 months of political turmoil, President Pervez Musharraf is so unpopular that his own party is distancing itself from the president, candidates and party officials admit.

2008-02-16.title: Russia Warns It May Back Breakaway Republics in Georgia
2008-02-16.abstract: Russia held a meeting with separatists leaders of in Georgia, and vowed to increase support for the them if Kosovo declares its independence and is recognized by the West.

2008-02-16.title: Germany: German-Pakistani Accused of Supporting Al Qaeda
2008-02-16.abstract: The police in Germany arrested a citizen of Pakistani origin on suspicion of acting as a courier of money, night-vision equipment and radios to Al Qaeda in the Afghan-Pakistani border area.

2008-02-16.title: Suicide Blast at Campaign Rally in Pakistan Kills 37
2008-02-16.abstract: The blast two days before parliamentary elections hit a rally organized by a candidate affiliated with Benazir Bhutto’s party.

2008-02-16.title: Kenya Peace Talks Stall on Power-Sharing Issues
2008-02-16.abstract: The country’s rival political parties have agreed on a few minor issues, but the big problems that were supposed to be solved by now seem as nettlesome as ever.

2008-02-17.title: Mexico City Bomber’s Motive Unclear, Police Say
2008-02-17.abstract: Investigators believe the bomber intended to plant his device at the police headquarters, about 300 yards from where he was found, but it went off in his hand.

2008-02-17.title: For Israel, Gaza Offers a Range of Risky Choices
2008-02-17.abstract: Gaza, controlled by Hamas, is officially committed to the destruction of Israel, the very country obligated to provide it with fuel, electricity, water and food.

2008-02-17.title: Baba Amte, 93, Dies; Advocate for Lepers
2008-02-17.abstract: Mr. Amte, a follower of Gandhi, gave up an early life of privilege to work for the poor, his direction irrevocably determined by an encounter with a destitute leper.

2008-02-17.title: Bush, in Africa, Emphasizes Successes Over Conflicts
2008-02-17.abstract: President Bush defended his decision not to visit violence-stricken nations like Kenya and Sudan, saying he wanted to focus on his programs to fight AIDS and malaria.

2008-02-17.title: In a Sultanate Known as Solo, One Too Many Kings
2008-02-17.abstract: Four years after the death of King Pakubowono XII, who left no heir and no instructions about his succession, the sultanate is no closer to determining who is its legitimate king.

2008-02-18.title: Iran Hangs 2 on Murder and Rape Charges
2008-02-18.abstract: The number of executions has sharply increased in Iran in the past year. Last year 298 people were executed, and 36 have been executed so far in 2008.

2008-02-18.title: Tanzania Welcomes Bush, but Obama Is Topic No. 1 on the Streets
2008-02-18.abstract: On a five-country tour to spotlight American efforts to fight poverty and disease in Africa, President Bush cannot escape the race to succeed him.

2008-02-18.title: Kosovo Declares Its Independence From Serbia
2008-02-18.abstract: Greeted with euphoria by ethnic Albanians, the move brings to a climax a showdown with Serbia and Russia.

2008-02-18.title: Vote Is Expected to Further Weaken Musharraf
2008-02-18.abstract: Pakistanis began voting in parliamentary elections on Monday amid anxiety over further political turmoil and violence.

2008-02-18.title: President-Elect of South Korea Is Questioned in Stock Scam
2008-02-18.abstract: President-elect Lee Myung-bak, who will be inaugurated on Feb. 25, was elected in part on promises that he would improve the country’s economy.

2008-02-19.title: Kosovo Declares Independence
2008-02-19.abstract: The breakaway province of Kosovo declared independence from Serbia, a moment celebrated by tens of thousands of ethnic Albanians.

2008-02-19.title: Rice, in Nairobi, Offers Incentives to End Violence
2008-02-19.abstract: Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice said Monday that the leaders of Kenya’s rival political factions must move faster to accept a power-sharing deal.

2008-02-19.title: Britain: Life Sentence in Beheading Plot
2008-02-19.abstract: Parviz Khan, the Birmingham man who pleaded guilty last month to plotting to kidnap and behead a British Muslim soldier, was sentenced to life in prison. Described by witnesses as a keen cricketer and pubgoer until contacts with Islamic militants radicalized him on a trip to Pakistan, he was ordered to serve at least 14 years for the plot, and for supplying militants in Afghanistan with money and equipment. The plot was uncovered by Britain’s security services. In secret recordings played in court, Mr. Khan was heard teaching his 5-year-old son to carry out a beheading. “How do you cut them with a knife?” he said to the boy. “Show me. Like this.” At another point, he asked the boy, “Who do you love?” The boy replied, “I love Sheik Osama bin Laden.” Mr. Khan then asked, “Who do you kill?” The boy’s reply: “America kill. Bush I kill, and Blair kill.”.

2008-02-19.title: Suicide Attack Kills 36 in Afghanistan
2008-02-19.abstract: The second serious attack in southern Afghanistan in two days also injured several Canadian soldiers.

2008-02-19.title: Do Not Rank
2008-02-19.abstract: After a long illness, Fidel Castro stepped down on Tuesday, ending one of the longest tenures as head of state in the world, according to Granma, the official publication of the Cuban Communist Party.

2008-02-20.title: Tape Inquiry: Ex-Spymaster in the Middle
2008-02-20.abstract: The actions of a key figure in a controversy over destroyed interrogation tapes show the C.I.A.’s protective instinct.

2008-02-20.title: Tackling a Society’s Boundaries, on TV and in a Family
2008-02-20.abstract: When it is first broadcast later this month, “Ippadikku Rose” (“Yours, Rose”) is expected to cause a sensation, introducing India’s first transgender celebrity to television.

2008-02-20.title: Bush Welcomes Castro’s Resignation
2008-02-20.abstract: President Bush said Tuesday that the resignation of Fidel Castro “ought to be a period of democratic transition” for Cuba.

2008-02-20.title: Pakistan Victors Want Dialogue With Militants
2008-02-20.abstract: The winners of the parliamentary elections said that they would take a new approach to fighting Islamic militants.

2008-02-20.title: Abbas and Olmert Struggle to Move Talks Forward
2008-02-20.abstract: The Israeli and Palestinian leaders met on Tuesday amid growing dissonance over the content of their talks and the kind of agreement they are trying to achieve.

2008-02-21.title: Bush Confronts Hard Questions in Ghana
2008-02-21.abstract: President Bush faced skepticism on Wednesday about American military policy and his AIDS initiative.

2008-02-21.title: Pakistan’s Victors May Lack Strength to Oust Musharraf
2008-02-21.abstract: A coalition government would need a two-thirds majority in order to remove President Pervez Musharraf or change the Constitution.

2008-02-21.title: Deadly Bomb in Mexico Was Meant for the Police
2008-02-21.abstract: The man carrying the bomb was linked to drug traffickers, investigators said.

2008-02-21.title: Opposition in Kenya Threatens More Protests
2008-02-21.abstract: Mediators from both sides remain in heated talks about how to lift the country out of a post-election crisis that has claimed more than 1,000 lives and destabilized the country.

2008-02-21.title: Files for Suicide Bombers Show No Down Syndrome
2008-02-21.abstract: Psychiatric case files of the bombers who killed nearly 100 people on Feb. 1 showed they suffered from depression and schizophrenia, but not Down Syndrome, as officials had suggested.

2008-02-22.title: The Week Ahead
2008-02-22.abstract: Alison Smale, of the International Herald Tribune, discusses events in the world this week.

2008-02-22.title: Security Council Weighs New Sanctions on Iran
2008-02-22.abstract: The backers of the new measure, Britain and France, said the draft would be open for comment, ahead of a report by the U.N. nuclear watchdog to be released on Friday.

2008-02-22.title: Former Concentration Camp Guard Dies
2008-02-22.abstract: Erna Wallisch, a former concentration camp guard who was under investigation for war crimes, died in a Vienna hospital last week, Austrian prosecutors said. Investigators had reopened the case against the 86-year-old woman earlier this year after new evidence from Polish witnesses suggested that she was involved in the murder of inmates in the Majdanek concentration camp near the city of Lublin, Poland. Mrs. Wallisch, who ranked seventh on the Simon Wiesenthal Center’s list of most-wanted Nazi war criminals, evaded prosecution in the 1970s due to a lack of evidence linking her directly to Nazi genocide.

2008-02-22.title: Fraud Unit Seeks Europe Report
2008-02-22.abstract: An internal report of the European Parliament is said to have revealed large-scale abuse in the use of funding by some deputies.

2008-02-22.title: Agency Confronts Iran With Evidence on Weapons
2008-02-22.abstract: Iran dismissed evidence suggesting that it had experimented with technology to make a nuclear weapon.

2008-02-23.title: Argentina's Cheap Drug Scourge
2008-02-23.abstract: Paco, a cheap and highly addictive smokable cocaine residue, has destroyed thousands of lives in Argentina. But Bilma Acuna is fighting back.

2008-02-23.title: Argentina's Cheap Drug Scourge
2008-02-23.abstract: Paco, a highly addictive smokable cocaine,  has destroyed thousands of lives in Argentina. But Bilma Acuna is fighting  back.

2008-02-23.title: Nuclear Agency Says Iran Has Used New Technology
2008-02-23.abstract: Officials with the U.N. nuclear watchdog said Iran had refused to deal with the evidence that served as the basis for American charges that Iran had tried to design a weapon.

2008-02-23.title: Cheap Cocaine Floods Argentina, Devouring Lives
2008-02-23.abstract: A highly addictive, smokable cocaine residue called paco has caused a cycle of drug-induced street violence.

2008-02-23.title: Rescuers Find Wreckage of Plane Carrying 46 in Venezuela
2008-02-23.abstract: Officials believe all 43 passengers and 3 crew members have perished in the accident, which occurred shortly after takeoff.

2008-02-24.title: U.S. Defense Chief and Australians Insist Alliance Is Strong
2008-02-24.abstract: Robert M. Gates and top Australian officials insisted that the security alliance had not frayed despite Australia’s recent pledge to remove all combat forces from Iraq.

2008-02-24.title: Putin’s Iron Grip on Russia Suffocates Opponents
2008-02-24.abstract: Behind a facade of democracy in Russia lies a centralized authority that is not reluctant to swat down challengers.

2008-02-24.title: Missile Strike by Israelis in Gaza Kills 3 Palestinians
2008-02-24.abstract: It was not immediately clear whether the men who were killed were civilians or combatants and no militant group immediately claimed them as members.

2008-02-24.title: 40 Killed in Blast Amid Crowd of Shiites in Iraq
2008-02-24.abstract: Shiites making a pilgrimage to the Shrine of Imam Husayn in Karbila were targeted by a suicide bomber.

2008-02-24.title: Amsterdam Tries Upscale Fix for Red-Light District Crime
2008-02-24.abstract: The planned gentrification of the red-light district was not caused by a wave of prudishness, but to drive out criminal gangs that have encroached on the area.

2008-02-25.title: Israel Adds Border Forces Ahead of Protest in Gaza
2008-02-25.abstract: Residents of the Gaza Strip have been called on to form a human chain on Monday along the Gaza side, to protest an Israeli embargo imposed since June.

2008-02-25.title: A Battle of Legal Briefs Rages Over ’07 Nigerian Vote
2008-02-25.abstract: A tribunal is set to rule on whether Nigeria’s presidential election last April should stand, with its violence and fraud so severe that international observers said it was not credible.

2008-02-25.title: In Argentina, No Assistance From Region on Gas Needs
2008-02-25.abstract: Argentina and Brazil are facing the possibility of short-term energy crises from a lack of natural gas needed to fuel industries and generate electricity for residents.

2008-02-25.title: Suicide Bomber Kills 40 at Iraq Highway Rest Stop
2008-02-25.abstract: Shiite Muslims making a pilgrimage to the Shrine of Imam Hussein in Karbala were targeted by a suicide bomber.

2008-02-25.title: Chinese Submarine Fleet Is Growing, Analysts Say
2008-02-25.abstract: Western military analysts estimated that China has more than 30 advanced submarines, and may have more submarines than the U.S. by the end of the decade.

2008-02-26.title: Taliban Threatens Afghan Cellphone Companies
2008-02-26.abstract: The Taliban has demanded that cellphone companies in Afghanistan cease operating at night because American forces are tracking militants through their mobile phones.

2008-02-26.title: Police Said to Have Assaulted Rights Lawyer in China
2008-02-26.abstract: One of China’s best-known human rights lawyers has been repeatedly beaten by police officers in recent days, according to his wife and associates.

2008-02-26.title: In Visit, Cellist’s Quest for Lost Chord to His Youth
2008-02-26.abstract: Now in Pyongyang as a cellist with the New York Philharmonic, Valentin Hirsu is asking about the fate of three Korean boys from his music school in Romania.

2008-02-26.title: Russian Candidate Denounces Kosovo
2008-02-26.abstract: The presumptive successor to Vladimir V. Putin committed to long-term support of Serbia against an independent Kosovo.

2008-02-26.title: Blast Kills Surgeon General of Pakistani Army
2008-02-26.abstract: A top Pakistani Army general was killed Monday when a suicide attacker approached his car at a traffic light in Rawalpindi and blew himself up.

2008-02-27.title: The Akhdam of Yemen
2008-02-27.abstract: In Yemen, the Akhdam, a word meaning "servants," live in extreme poverty and at the absolute margins of Yemeni society.

2008-02-27.title: Binding Emissions Treaty Still a Possibility, U.S. Says
2008-02-27.abstract: A senior White House official on Tuesday outlined a new tactic aimed at convincing a skeptical Europe that the Bush administration would support a meaningful agreement to limit global warming.

2008-02-27.title: Warrantless Searches Removed From Legislation in Mexico
2008-02-27.abstract: Mexican lawmakers stripped a provision from their plan to overhaul the country’s judiciary that would have given police officers the ability to enter homes without obtaining warrants beforehand.

2008-02-27.title: Dan Shomron, Leader of Entebbe Airport Rescue, Is Dead at 70
2008-02-27.abstract: Mr. Shomron was in command of the Israeli paratroopers in the 1976 hostage rescue at Entebbe International Airport in Uganda.

2008-02-27.title: North Korea Seeks a Clapton Concert
2008-02-27.abstract: On Tuesday North Korean officials invited the superstar rock guitarist Eric Clapton to perform there, according to a representative for the North Korean Embassy in London.

2008-02-28.title: Ask Russians About Their Presidential Election
2008-02-28.abstract: Last week, The New York Times created a Russian-language Live Journal community to give Russian readers a chance to read and discuss articles written by the Moscow bureau. So far, Russian readers have posted more than 4,000 comments.

2008-02-28.title: Russia’s Next President
2008-02-28.abstract: Dmitri A. Medvedev is set to become the new president of Russia, having been personally chosen for the job by President Vladimir V. Putin.

2008-02-28.title: Putin’s Chosen Successor Whatzisname
2008-02-28.abstract: Asked to speak about Dimitri Medvedev during the Democratic debate Tuesday night, neither candidates appeared to know much about Vladimir Putin’s likely successor as president of Russia.

2008-02-28.title: Dmitri Medvedev, in His Own Words
2008-02-28.abstract: Excerpts of speeches by and interviews with Dmitri A. Medvedev, President Vladimir V. Putin’s hand-picked successor, as posted in English on his campaign Web site.

2008-02-28.title: Hamas and Israelis Trade Attacks, Killing Several
2008-02-28.abstract: In a sudden escalation of violence, an Israeli civilian was killed Wednesday in a rocket attack by Hamas militants from Gaza.

2008-02-29.title: Global Seed Vault
2008-02-29.abstract: Governments and scientists have created a global network of plant banks to preserve seeds and sprouts from around the world.

2008-02-29.title: China to Reconsider One-Child Limit
2008-02-29.abstract: Any changes would come gradually and would not mean an elimination of family planning policies, a senior official said.

2008-02-29.title: Iraq Clears Execution of ‘Chemical Ali’
2008-02-29.abstract: Iraqi leaders found a way out of a legal dilemma that had slowed the approval of Ali Hassan al-Majid’s execution.

2008-02-29.title: Israel Sees Escalation in Gazans’ Longer-Range Strikes
2008-02-29.abstract: Four rockets fired by Palestinian militants in Gaza reached the Israeli coastal city of Ashkelon. Israeli airstrikes in Gaza killed at least 11 people.

2008-02-29.title: Sweden and Norway Hold 6 on Terror-Related Charges
2008-02-29.abstract: In coordinated raids, six people were arrested on suspicion of financing or otherwise taking part in terrorist activity.
